title: Two Europes in Five Minutes
content:
Within a few minutes of each other yesterday in Strasbourg, Members of the European Parliament voted through two motions relevant to how we regulate knowledge creation, sharing and application to support research and innovation. Neither has any immediate legal impact, but still set a direction that may well shape future, legislative, measures, for better or for worse.

And that direction is… confused.

The first to be voted on was a report on Generative AI and Copyright, prepared on the initiative of the Parliament’s Legal Affairs Committee, and led by Axel Voss MEP. The second was a
creator: Stephen Wyber
sourceDescription: We advocate for your right to access and share knowledge!

title: The UK’s Copyright Laws Will Hobble Its AI Ambitions
content:
Guest blog post by Benjamin White

Originally published on 11 December 2025 on the Times Higher Education

Artificial intelligence played a crucial role in enabling the safe and rapid development and roll-out of the Covid vaccine. Using scientific journal articles, published clinical trials, databases and other resources, scientists were able to use machine learning models to quickly prioritise fruitful research pathways, allowing them to bypass traditional bottlenecks associated with vaccine development and enhance the precision of antigen
